title: Facility Graph And Scenario Expansion
scope: Implement explicit facility graph/pathing/travel duration, named deterministic scenarios, scenario JSON validation/round trip, and save/load expansion while preserving baseline behavior.
inputs: Focused target files named by story packets, expected around src/adeptus_testus/facility.py and tests/test_facility.py
outputs: tactical plan for graph and scenario work
bounded story packets for graph/pathing, scenarios, serialization, and save/load compatibility
acceptance_criteria: Facility graph represents all seven required rooms, validates connectivity, computes deterministic travel duration/routes, and rejects invalid rooms or impossible routes predictably.
Default scenario remains compatible and deterministic, and alternate scenario maintenance_day exists and runs deterministically.
Scenario JSON serialization/deserialization round trip works, invalid scenario JSON or invalid room references fail predictably, and save/load preserves relevant expanded state.
suggested_story_boundaries: Graph/pathing/travel-time behavior with direct unit tests.
Named scenario model and maintenance_day behavior with deterministic execution tests.
Scenario JSON and expanded save/load compatibility tests.
non_goals: Do not implement incidents, operations reports, README updates, or expanded report CLI beyond minimal hooks needed for graph/scenario behavior.
Do not introduce randomness, wall-clock time, GUI, or runtime dependencies.
Do not perform broad refactors unrelated to graph/scenario contracts.
dependency_summary: Requires m01 baseline contract or summary to preserve compatibility expectations. Later incident/report/CLI milestone depends on this milestone's graph and scenario behavior.
change_boundary: Expected product files: src/adeptus_testus/facility.py and tests/test_facility.py; additional small module files are allowed only if a story packet justifies clearer boundaries.
CLI changes should be minimal unless needed to preserve existing --until behavior.
No installed Adeptus skill changes or prior run artifact rewrites.
test_policy: Each story may add or update tests only for its acceptance criteria. Preserve baseline tests and add focused graph, scenario, JSON, and save/load tests.
validation_expectations: Run focused pytest during stories where useful.
Milestone completion should include MCP syntax check and bounded pytest evidence.
Cleanup runtime cache/temp artifacts before handoff.
context_rules: Lexmechanic reads this contract, intake-summary, and m01 milestone summary only.
Enginseer reads assigned story packet plus focused files listed in that packet.
Sibling story details are not live context unless summarized as dependency contracts.
